question.text: Under Regulation 8A of SEBI ICDR, a shareholder holding more than 20% of the pre-issue share capital can sell through OFS in an IPO up to what maximum percentage of their pre-issue holding?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 8A specifies that shareholders holding more than 20% of the pre-issue share capital can sell up to 50% of their pre-issue holding through OFS in an IPO. For shareholders with less than 20%, the limit is 10% of the pre-issue share capital. **The Trap:** 10% is the limit for shareholders with less than 20% holding, not for those with more than 20%. Candidates must carefully note which threshold applies to which category of shareholder. **Cross-Exam Context:** OFS limits are specific to NISM Series IX but understanding dilution concepts is relevant across NISM Series V-A and Series VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** These limits include pre-IPO secondary transfers. Always account for any shares already transferred by the promoter in secondary transactions before computing the available OFS room.
